prompt: Which city in the world do you find most fascinating to think about, and why? Pick one and commit to it.
response: If I had to pick one city, I'd say Venice, Italy. There's just something captivating about a city built entirely on water, with canals instead of streets and boats instead of cars. The architecture, history and culture are all so unique and evocative. Imagining what it must have been like in its heyday as a wealthy trading empire is fascinating to me. The idea of a city unchanged for centuries, slowly sinking into the lagoon, is hauntingly beautiful. And of course, the art, music, food and festivals like Carnival make it an endlessly intriguing place. Venice is unlike anywhere else in the world.
